title: Thiruppathisaram
tagline: Thiruppathisaram — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Thiruppathisaram is a village in the Kanyakumari District of Tamil Nadu, the southern Indian state.
overview.description: Thiruppathisaram is a village in the Kanyakumari District of Tamil Nadu, the southern Indian state. This village is renowned for the Thiruvazhmarban Temple dedicated (Vishnu).

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Thiruvazhmarban temple
topPlaces.description: The Thiruvazhmarban Temple, also known as Sri Kuralappa Perumal Temple in Thiruppathisaram, a village in Kanyakumari district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, is dedicated to the Hindu god Vishnu. Thiruvazhmarban Temple is located about 5 km far, north-easterly to Nagercoil, 20 km far, north-westerly to Kanyakumari and 45 km south-west of Nanguneri Divya Desam.

topPlaces.name: Putheri
topPlaces.description: Putheri is a village panchayat located in Rajakkamangalam block of Kanyakumari district, Tamil Nadu, India. The panchayat has total of seven panchayat constituencies.

topPlaces.name: Vadasery
topPlaces.description: Vadasery is an area/village of Nagercoil city in Kanyakumari district, Tamil Nadu, India. It is also famous for its Christopher (mofussil) bus stand, one of the two bus termini in Nagercoil is located here.

topPlaces.name: Ozhuginasery
topPlaces.description: Ozhuginasery is a neighbourhood in Kanyakumari district of Tamil Nadu state in the peninsular India. Ozhuginasery is located at an altitude of about 67 m above the mean sea level with the geographical coordinates of 8.1895°N 77.4379°E / 8.1895; 77.4379.

topPlaces.name: Nagaraja Temple, Nagercoil
topPlaces.description: Nagaraja Temple is an early large temple found in the city of Nagercoil (Nagarkōyil) near the southern tip of Tamil Nadu, India. Its dating is uncertain but likely pre-12th-century.

topPlaces.name: Erachakulam
topPlaces.description: Erachakulam is a village near Nagercoil in Kanyakumari district of Tamil Nadu state in India. Erachakulam is located at an altitude of about 55 m above the mean sea level with the geographical coordinates of 8.229100°N 77.430600°E / 8.229100; 77.430600 (i.e., 8°13'44.8"N, 77°25'50.2"E).

topPlaces.name: Meenakshipuram, Nagercoil
topPlaces.description: Meenakshipuram is a neighbourhood of Nagercoil, in Kanniyakumari district of Tamil Nadu state in the peninsular India. It is located at an altitude of 46 m above the mean sea level with the geographical coordinates of 8°10′57.4″N 77°26′08.6″E (i.e., 8.182611°N, 77.435708°E).

topPlaces.name: Vadiveeswaram
topPlaces.description: Vadiveeswaram is a village, now part of the town of Nagercoil, in Kanyakumari district in the southern Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The village was originally an agraharam, or a traditional double line of houses occupied by Brahmins and flanking a temple or pair of temples.

items.note: The Thiruvazhmarban Temple, also known as Sri Kuralappa Perumal Temple in Thiruppathisaram, a village in Kanyakumari district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, is dedicated to the Hindu god Vishnu. Thiruvazhmarban Temple is located about 5 km far, north-easterly to Nagercoil, 20 km far, north-westerly to Kanyakumari and 45 km south-west of Nanguneri Divya Desam.
Putheri is a village panchayat located in Rajakkamangalam block of Kanyakumari district, Tamil Nadu, India. The panchayat has total of seven panchayat constituencies.

Vadasery is an area/village of Nagercoil city in Kanyakumari district, Tamil Nadu, India. It is also famous for its Christopher (mofussil) bus stand, one of the two bus termini in Nagercoil is located here.

Ozhuginasery is a neighbourhood in Kanyakumari district of Tamil Nadu state in the peninsular India. Ozhuginasery is located at an altitude of about 67 m above the mean sea level with the geographical coordinates of 8.1895°N 77.4379°E / 8.1895; 77.4379.
Nagaraja Temple is an early large temple found in the city of Nagercoil (Nagarkōyil) near the southern tip of Tamil Nadu, India. Its dating is uncertain but likely pre-12th-century.

Vadiveeswaram is a village, now part of the town of Nagercoil, in Kanyakumari district in the southern Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The village was originally an agraharam, or a traditional double line of houses occupied by Brahmins and flanking a temple or pair of temples.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Thiruppathisaram?
faq.a: The best time to visit Thiruppathisaram is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

The nearest airport is Thiruvananthapuram International Airport (66 km) and the nearest railway station is Thiruvananthapuram Central (63 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Thiruppathisaram?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Thiruppathisaram at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Thiruppathisaram?
faq.a: Stays in Thiruppathisaram start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Thiruppathisaram?
faq.a: The top attractions are Thiruvazhmarban temple, Putheri, Vadasery, Ozhuginasery, Nagaraja Temple, Nagercoil.
